Rule Description: Every governed object shall have a unique stable ID using the approved prefix pattern.
Every governed object shall include ID name object type owner status version and review frequency.
Every active requirement shall map to at least one active control.
Every active control shall map to at least one evidence object and one system of record.
Evidence used for active control testing shall be current within the defined review period.
Material technology changes shall have an approved ARB decision record before implementation.
Every governed application shall map to at least one business capability and one accountable owner.
Every business service shall map to supporting technical services where applicable.
Every governed CI shall have an owner lifecycle state and authoritative source.
Every exception shall have an expiration date remediation owner and compensating control.
Every waiver shall identify approving body scope rationale and review date.
Technology lifecycle state shall use approved values only.
Every critical requirement shall map to at least one regulatory or framework driver unless explicitly classified as internal management practice.
Control frequency shall use approved values and align to risk criticality.
Every governance remediation work item shall reference source requirement control or exception ID.
Every governance metric shall identify source system calculation logic owner and cadence.
Every standard shall map to at least one requirement or documented advisory classification.
Every approved solution pattern shall map to at least one architecture principle and one standard domain.
Third-party technology records shall link to architecture review and risk assessment evidence when material.
AI and emerging technology submissions shall link to AI governance review and applicable model risk evidence.
